title: PRA: Autopsy and coroner’s reports

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: PRA: Autopsy and coroner’s reports
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act (Government Code Section 6250 et seq.) ("CPRA”), and all other applicable laws, please disclose the following records.

Any and all autopsy, death investigation, and coroner’s reports relating to the death of:

Name: Alfredo Flores-Bravo, Date of birth: 04-04-1965, Date of death: 07-05-2011

If records pursuant to this request are possessed in machine-readable format such as an .xls, .csv spreadsheet file, please provide the record in this format. Please disclose these records in the format in which they are kept, e.g., .ppt, .pptx, .doc, or .docx.

The fundamental rule of the CPRA is a presumption of public access. “In other words, [A]ll public records are subject to disclosure unless the Legislature has expressly provided to the contrary.” Williams v. Superior Court, 5 Cal. 4th 337 (1993). This presumption finds further support in the California Constitution, as amended by Proposition 59 in 2004. “A statute, court rule, or other authority, including those in effect on the effective date of this subdivision, shall be broadly construed if it furthers the people's right of access, and narrowly construed if it limits the right of access.” Cal. Const. Art. 1 § 3(b)(2).

Please provide a determination on this request within 10 days of your receipt of it. If you determine that any or all of the information qualifies for an exemption from disclosure, please note whether, as is normally the case, the exemption is discretionary, and if so whether it is necessary in this case to exercise your discretion to withhold the information. If you determine that some but not all of the information is exempt from disclosure and that you intend to withhold it, please redact it for the time being and make the rest available as requested. In any event, please provide a signed notification citing the legal authorities on which you rely if you determine that any or all of the information is exempt and will not be disclosed.

Please apply a fee waiver to this request. If the request for a fee waiver is denied, please provide notification of any duplication costs exceeding $50 before you duplicate the records. If the request for a fee waiver is denied, please further provide an index of all other requests since 2018 for which a fee waiver was denied, and the reason therefore.

Please respond to this request and disclose the records electronically by email to this MuckRock email address. Please let us know if you have any questions or if we can help ensure this request is the least burdensome possible for your agency while providing a correct and full disclosure in the public interest.


Thanks so much, 

Dillon Bergin

title: Standard Records Request — Baltimore

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Standard Records Request — Baltimore
communications.communication:
April 3, 2026

Re: Public Records Request — Holiday Lighting Procurement Records

Dear Xavier A. Conaway,

Pursuant to the Maryland Public Information Act (PIA) (Md. Code, GP § 4-101), I am writing to request access to and copies of the following public records from Baltimore:

all records related to holiday lighting and seasonal decorative lighting services, including but not limited to: contracts, purchase orders, invoices, bid submissions, vendor proposals, requests for proposals (RFPs), requests for qualifications (RFQs), scope of work documents, vendor selection criteria, evaluation scoring sheets, and any correspondence with vendors or contractors related to holiday or seasonal lighting services.

This request covers the past five (5) fiscal years.

As you may be aware, the Maryland Public Information Act (PIA) requires a response within 10 days of receipt of this request.

I would prefer to receive these records in electronic format (PDF, Excel, or other digital format) delivered via email to rfp@britenites.com. If electronic delivery is not available, please advise on alternative arrangements.

Regarding fees: I request a fee waiver as this information is being sought in the public interest. If a fee waiver is not available, please provide a cost estimate before proceeding with the request if anticipated fees will exceed $25.00.

If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific statutory exemption and provide any non-exempt portions that can be reasonably segregated.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this request. Please feel free to contact me at rfp@britenites.com if you have any questions or need clarification.

Sincerely,
Dillon Williams
rfp@britenites.com

title: Community Oriented Policing Services (COPS) grants awarded to the Los Angeles Police Department (LAPD) (Department of Justice, Community Oriented Policing Services (COPS))

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, I am requesting records relating to Community Oriented Policing Services (COPS) grants awarded to the Los Angeles Police Department (LAPD), City of Los Angeles, California, for the period January 1, 1994 through December 31, 1997.

Specifically, I request:

1/ All grant award documents, grant agreements, and cooperative agreements between the COPS Office and the Los Angeles Police Department or the City of Los Angeles executed between January 1, 1994 and December 31, 1997, including but not limited to grants awarded under the COPS Ahead, COPS FAST, COPS MORE, and Universal Hiring Program (UHP) grant programs.

2/ All documents stating the number of officer positions funded by each grant award to the LAPD during this period, including any documentation of the total authorized officer positions and the grant's contribution to those positions.

3/ All documents stating the conditions, matching requirements, maintenance-of-effort obligations, and community policing implementation requirements attached to each grant award to the LAPD during this period.

4/ Any progress reports, compliance reports, or monitoring reports submitted by the LAPD or the City of Los Angeles to the COPS Office pursuant to any grant award during this period.

5/ Any correspondence between the COPS Office and the LAPD or the City of Los Angeles regarding grant conditions, compliance, or officer hiring targets during this period.

I am a journalist and author researching a book about the 1997 North Hollywood bank shootout and its institutional context. I request a fee waiver on the grounds that disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest because it is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of the operations and activities of the federal government, specifically the implementation of the 1994 Violent Crime Control and Law Enforcement Act's officer-hiring mandate and its effect on a major American police department. The information is not sought for commercial use.

If a fee waiver is not granted, please notify me before incurring costs in excess of $25.

If any portion of this request is denied, please release all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions and provide a written explanation of the basis for any withholding, citing the specific FOIA exemption(s) claimed.

I am available to clarify or narrow this request if it would assist processing. Please confirm receipt.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Daniel Brown

title: BWC & Incident Records: Juan Ayon-Barraza Kidnapping Pursuit & Fatal Officer-Involved Shooting – Richmond, CA (April 2020)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the fatal officer-involved shooting of kidnapping suspect Juan Ayon-Barraza following a high-speed multi-agency pursuit that ended in Richmond.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: April 16, 2020
Location: South 37th Street and Stege Avenue / Carlson Boulevard, Richmond, California
Suspect Name: Juan Carlos Ayon-Barraza, 24, of Vacaville/Vallejo
Victim: Tanisha Thomas, 29, of Vallejo (kidnapping victim)
Involved Officers: Richmond PD Officer Deorian; Oakland PD Officers Recendez and Hohn
Agencies Involved: Richmond Police Department, Oakland Police Department, Moraga Police Department, Contra Costa County Sheriff's Office, California Highway Patrol
 
Incident Summary: On the morning of April 16, 2020, Vallejo Police received a missing person report for 29-year-old Tanisha Thomas, who was last seen with Ayon-Barraza. That evening at approximately 6:45 p.m., a cyclist called 911 reporting a man in a white Chevrolet Astro van dumping what appeared to be a body on the side of the road near Pinehurst Road and Skyline Boulevard in unincorporated Contra Costa County. Responding officers from Oakland, Moraga, and the Contra Costa County Sheriff's Office located the victim, who had been shot in the head and severely beaten. The suspect's van was spotted and a pursuit ensued, reaching speeds over 100 mph on Interstate 580 and Highway 13. The 20-minute chase ended in Richmond at South 37th Street and Stege Avenue, where Ayon-Barraza made a U-turn and rammed his van into Oakland police vehicles, injuring two officers. Officers from Richmond and Oakland fired on the suspect after he pointed a firearm in their direction. Ayon-Barraza sustained a fatal gunshot wound and was pronounced dead at the scene. Thomas died approximately one month later from her injuries.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from Richmond Police Officer Deorian and any other Richmond PD officers who responded to or were involved in the pursuit and shooting.
 
All BWC footage from Oakland Police Officers Recendez and Hohn and any other Oakland PD officers present during the pursuit and final confrontation.
 
All dashboard camera (dashcam) footage from all Richmond PD and Oakland PD patrol vehicles involved in the pursuit and at the shooting scene.
 
All helicopter or aerial camera footage captured during the pursuit.
 
The complete incident report from the Richmond Police Department, including all officer narratives and supplemental reports.
 
All 911 call recordings related to this incident, including the initial call from the cyclist reporting the body being dumped and any calls from the pursuit and shooting scene.
 
All dispatch/CAD (Computer-Aided Dispatch) logs related to the pursuit and officer-involved shooting.
 
Any use-of-force reports filed by officers who discharged their weapons.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Andrej Ilic

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All expenses and all revenues for the Detroit Board of Zoning Appeals for Fiscal Years 2023 through 2025, with special attention to all line item purchases made to Precision Reporting (DBA Remote Legal) and each revenue transaction.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

DetroitTaxpayer
